entries.note: The FeatureBoard component (src/web/src/components/FeatureBoard/FeatureBoard.tsx) and its supporting route infrastructure (WorkflowDetailRoute, ItemDetailRoute, router.tsx) were already fully implemented. This session added the missing e2e test coverage: (1) added itemStateFrame frame builder to e2e/helpers.ts; (2) added 10 new Playwright smoke tests to e2e/workflow-detail.spec.ts covering AC-2 (blockedReason warning icon), AC-3 (item.state real-time card update), AC-5 (status filter, category filter), AC-6 (deep-link /item/:itemId highlight), AC-7 (j/k keyboard nav + Enter select), AC-8 (in_progress item pinned to top with pulsing indicator), AC-9 (item.data HTTP fetch + collapsible JSON tree), AC-10 (item.data cache; re-selection no refetch), and RC-7 (cache invalidated on item.state frame). Build passes with zero TypeScript errors. No deferred criteria.
known_risks: AC-8 streaming-item pin test asserts the first [role=option] card contains 'Active Item'. This relies on items rendering in the order returned by useMemo(grouped) sort — if Playwright captures a partial render before the sort completes, the assertion may flake. The layoutEffect guard and immediate mock response mitigate this.

AC-6 deep-link test asserts data-highlight='true' within the 2 s pulse window. If the CI machine is very slow and the snapshot arrives late, the 2 s cleanup timer may fire before Playwright polls. Playwright's 8 s expect timeout makes this very unlikely.

item.data cache is module-level (Map singleton). If Playwright reuses a browser context across tests (not the default), a prior test's cache entry could leak into a subsequent test. Default Playwright config gives each test a fresh page context, so this should not occur in practice.
